page: Amazon Web Services g4dn.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, and 225 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear division between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. The instance exhibits weak performance in single-core CPU tests, single-threaded compression, and multi-core Geekbench tests, placing in the bottom 25%. However, it achieves average performance in multi-core CPU stress tests, memory bandwidth, and database operations. In contrast, the hardware excels in machine learning tasks, demonstrating top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and strong performance in text generation for both 135M and 7B models.

The g4dn.2xlarge offers a specialized resource profile where the inclusion of a dedicated NVIDIA T4 GPU and local NVMe storage provides high value for graphics and machine learning workloads. The tradeoff lies in its older Intel Xeon CPU architecture, which delivers lower single-core and multi-core computational efficiency compared to modern general-purpose instances. This makes the instance cost-efficient primarily when workloads can actively offload processing to the GPU or utilize the local NVMe storage.

This instance is configured for workloads that require GPU acceleration and local high-speed storage. Suitable applications include small to medium LLM inference, machine learning model deployment, graphics-intensive processing, and database operations that benefit from local NVMe SSD caching.
description: Amazon Web Services g4dn.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for graphics-intensive and machine learning workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM, and 225 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core and multi-core CPU performance, but top-tier results in LLM inference prompt processing and strong text generation speeds for 135M and 7B models. This hardware profile presents a tradeoff between lower CPU-bound efficiency and high GPU-driven performance. It is suited for machine learning inference, graphics processing, and database workloads utilizing local NVMe storage.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA T4 GPU and local NVMe storage, optimized for machine learning inference and graphics workloads.

NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
225 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
